_comments: Remote-browser backend registry for the Remote Browser channel (/plans/realtime/realtime-bridges-architecture.md §4d). Each row is a backend that hosts a real, live browser the channel drives over CDP while an agent talks. ProviderType is SelfHost (MJ orchestrates a lightweight headless-Chrome container) or Service (a browser-as-a-service exposes a CDP connect endpoint). SupportedFeatures is an IRemoteBrowserProviderFeatures JSON string (omitted flag = not supported). DefaultControlMode (AgentOnly/ViewOnly/Collaborative) is the channel default, overridable per-channel and at runtime. DriverClass resolves the concrete BaseRemoteBrowserProvider subclass via ClassFactory. STATUS: all 'Active' — drivers ship in @memberjunction/remote-browser-* in this same program; like the bridge drivers, each binds its real provider SDK/credentials via a SetSdkFactory seam before production use. IDs are assigned by mj-sync on first push — primaryKey/sync intentionally omitted.

fields.Name: Self-Hosted Chrome
fields.Description: MJ-orchestrated lightweight headless-Chrome container (just Chrome + a CDP port — no heavy image). computer-use connects over CDP; the viewport is encoded from CDP screencast into the channel's ScreenOut track; human input is routed directly. No vendor cost, full control, no provider-native AI harness (we use our own computer-use control).

fields.Name: Browserbase
fields.Description: Browser-as-a-service. Exposes a CDP connect endpoint plus a first-party AI-control harness (Stagehand act/extract/observe), a live-view stream with human takeover, stealth, proxy egress, session recording, persistent contexts, and CAPTCHA solving. The richest backend — offload orchestration and streaming entirely.

fields.Name: Steel
fields.Description: Browser-as-a-service (steel.dev). CDP connect endpoint, a session viewer with human takeover, stealth, proxy egress, session recording, persistent contexts, and CAPTCHA solving. No first-party AI harness — driven by MJ computer-use (or OSS Stagehand-over-CDP) control strategies.

fields.Name: Browserless
fields.Description: Browser-as-a-service (browserless.io) — CDP-as-a-service with a live debug viewer, session recording, proxy egress, multi-tab, and downloads. Lean and inexpensive; defaults to ViewOnly (its viewer is debug-oriented rather than first-class human handoff).

fields.Name: Hyperbrowser
fields.Description: Browser-as-a-service (hyperbrowser.ai). CDP connect endpoint plus a first-party agentic browse API, live-view with human takeover, stealth, proxy egress, session recording, persistent contexts, multi-tab, downloads, and CAPTCHA solving.
